# Proof: PA's inbox action/provider registrations were dead duplicates at runtime

## 1. Core registration order (packages/core/src/runtime.ts, develop 5471346e7a6)

registerPlugin() executes strictly in this order (excerpted):

  ~line 1880:  if (pluginToRegister.init) { ... await pluginToRegister.init(config, this); ... }
  ~line 1902:  if (pluginToRegister.actions) { ... existingActionNames.has(action.name) ->
               "Skipping duplicate plugin action" ... }
  ~line 1923:  if (pluginToRegister.providers) { ... existingProviderNames.has(provider.name) ->
               "Skipping duplicate plugin provider" ... }

i.e. a plugin's `init()` runs BEFORE its own `actions`/`providers` arrays register.

## 2. PA init loads plugin-inbox first

plugins/plugin-personal-assistant/src/plugin.ts init():
  await ensureLifeOpsInboxPluginRegistered(runtime);   // registers the whole inboxPlugin
That call happens inside PA's init(), so by the time the runtime walks PA's own
`actions`/`providers` arrays, plugin-inbox has ALREADY registered:
  - the INBOX umbrella + INBOX_* promoted virtuals (post-port, promoted by
    plugin-inbox itself in plugins/plugin-inbox/src/plugin.ts), and
  - the `inboxTriage` provider.

Consequence (pre-change develop): PA's own `inboxTriageProvider` array entry and
`...promoteSubactionsToActions(inboxAction)` spread hit the duplicate-name guard
("Skipping duplicate plugin provider" / "Skipping duplicate plugin action") on
every boot — dead code. PA's local 179-line provider copy in
src/providers/inbox-triage.ts was never executed.

## 3. Post-change: single-source registration, observable in a real runtime

plugins/plugin-inbox/test/inbox-routes.real-runtime.test.ts registers the REAL
inboxPlugin STANDALONE (no PA) on a real PGLite AgentRuntime and asserts:

  - runtime.actions contains INBOX, INBOX_LIST, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_REPLY,
    INBOX_SNOOZE, INBOX_ARCHIVE, INBOX_APPROVE
  - runtime.providers contains inboxTriage + inboxCrossChannelContext

(pre-port, standalone plugin-inbox registered only the raw INBOX umbrella; the
INBOX_* virtuals existed only when PA was loaded — the standalone gap this port
closes). See test-plugin-inbox.txt for the green run.

## 4. Egress-redaction equivalence of the removed PA provider copy

PA's dead copy wrapped snippets/deep-links in LifeOps egress redaction. It built
its context as createLifeOpsEgressContext({ isOwner: true, ... }) and the
provider is owner-gated twice (roleGate.minRole OWNER + hasOwnerAccess()), so
every reachable call site redacted with an owner context — a pass-through. The
live plugin-inbox provider (also roleGate OWNER + hasOwnerAccess) is observably
identical for every reachable input.
